role_title: Senior Director, Enterprise Delivery and Growth

location.office_time_required: preference for proximity to Toronto for key cross-functional collaboration

secondary_archetype: Enterprise Customer Success / Delivery Leadership (VP/SVP-equivalent GM role)

level_signals: 5+ years managing Director-level leaders across distinct functional disciplines
Ownership of combined post-sale P&L with $200M+ ARR
Comp range up to $334,500 base in top-tier states, consistent with Senior Director / VP-equivalent

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 12+ years of enterprise software leadership spanning post-sale functions at a B2B SaaS or enterprise technology company.
Direct, accountable leadership of a professional services, implementation, or delivery organization, with demonstrated understanding of org design, quality drivers, and scale failure modes.
Direct, accountable leadership of a customer success or account management organization responsible for post-live growth, NRR, and expansion.
Ownership of a combined post-sale P&L with $200M+ ARR, accountable across the full customer lifecycle (implementation through renewal/expansion).
5+ years managing Director-level leaders across distinct functional disciplines, with a track record of setting standards and holding senior leaders accountable without micromanagement.
Proven success operating in a BD/Sales-led commercial environment and establishing shared norms across BD, Delivery, and CS without imposing rigid process.

Experience leading or directly managing a solutions architecture, technical account management, or professional services engineering function, with an understanding of how technical delivery teams complement commercial account teams on shared enterprise accounts.

Demonstrated ability to integrate technical and commercial organizations under a unified operating model — including solutions architecture, technical account management, and engineering functions alongside commercial success teams — while preserving functional depth and career paths within each discipline.
Deep familiarity with enterprise retail, grocery, or adjacent complex B2B domains, including how large retailers evaluate, implement, and scale technology solutions.

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Track record of improving time-to-live and implementation quality at scale, with measurable gains in launch speed, activation rates, or delivery consistency.
Experience transforming CS from reactive enablement to proactive business advisory, including talent strategy, operating model, and tooling changes.
Hands-on design of implementation-to-success motions where delivery and success run in parallel on the same accounts.

Experience running commercial success and technical account management as distinct disciplines within the same organization, with clear role boundaries, shared account ownership models, and escalation protocols.

Track record of building or scaling a TAM or technical success function — including defining the role boundary between TAM, solutions architecture, and support — with clear career ladders that attract and retain technical talent.
Background operating across multiple software business lines with distinct GTM and delivery motions and establishing cross-portfolio coordination standards.
Familiarity with tiered CS models (high-touch for strategic accounts and scaled/digital for the long tail) and when to apply each.
Experience applying AI to augment CS, TAM, and delivery workflows to scale coverage and quality without linear headcount growth.
Background in or strong exposure to grocery, retail technology, ecommerce platforms, or supply chain software.

scope.team_size_estimate: 3 Director-led organizations (Retailer Delivery, Enterprise Architecture & Engineering, Retailer Success); total headcount unclear
scope.surface: StorefrontPro, Enterprise API (Fulfillment and Ads), FoodStorm — full retailer post-sale lifecycle from implementation through renewal/expansion
scope.business_model: B2B SaaS / enterprise software — selling digital commerce infrastructure to large grocery and retail chains
core_responsibilities: Act as the leader for StorefrontPro, Enterprise API (Fulfillment and Ads), and FoodStorm, owning the software portfolio's business outcomes end to end
Lead and set the strategic agenda for three Director-led teams — Retailer Delivery, Enterprise Architecture & Engineering, and Retailer Success
Build a senior consultative CS capability by hiring and sponsoring Principal Growth Strategists
Define and operationalize the joint BD-CS operating model for enterprise accounts
Set the strategic standard for enterprise implementations and own time-to-live as a business lever
Design the implementation-to-success motion as an intentionally managed lifecycle
Lead the Enterprise Architecture & Engineering organization to drive technical delivery excellence
Represent the portfolio narrative to executive leadership

compensation.equity_form: new hire equity grant plus annual refresh grants

green_flags: Compensation range explicitly disclosed and highly competitive ($264K–$334.5K base depending on location)
Equity (new hire + annual refresh) explicitly mentioned
Role scope clearly defined with three distinct Director-led teams and named products
Specific metrics named (NRR, GTV, time-to-live, CSAT) indicating mature measurement culture
Remote-first (Flex First) policy with geographic flexibility across US and Canada
Role has executive visibility — presents to executive leadership and interfaces with retailer executives
red_flags: Significant experience bar (12+ years, $200M+ ARR P&L ownership) narrows the candidate pool considerably
Preference for Toronto proximity may disadvantage remote candidates far from Canada despite 'remote' designation
Broad scope across three distinct Director-led orgs may indicate under-structuring or turnaround situation — risk of overextension
No visa sponsorship information stated — unclear for non-US/Canada candidates
Role spans both commercial (NRR, expansion) and technical (solutions architecture, forward-deployed engineering) functions — unusually wide remit

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from comp range, org scope, and years requirements)
scope.team_size_estimate (headcount not stated; only org structure described)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (named in JD as outcomes but not formally labeled as KPIs)
scope.business_model (inferred from product descriptions and enterprise retailer context)
compensation.base_range_usd minimum/maximum (derived from lowest and highest state-tier ranges stated)
ambiguities: Location is tagged #LI-Remote and described as 'remote across the United States and Canada' but also states 'preference for proximity to Toronto' — treated as remote-us/Canada eligible with soft Toronto preference, not a hard onsite requirement
compensation.base_range_usd set to [$264,000, $334,500] representing the full spread across all US pay tiers; actual range depends on candidate's permanent work location
